violations.text: Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Dishes and utensils were removed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

Raw animal foods eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods chorizo in the 1 door reach-in. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Proper food storage reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by dead cockroach in the rinse compartment of the 3-bay sink. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to increase pest control as frequently as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026
The 3 door reach-in was observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the 1 door reach-in shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contast surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

The wall by the mop sink is constructed of wood. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Per PIC, wood wall was just put up and will be covered with FRP. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2026
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper towels were provided at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

Raw animal foods chicken and eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods chorizo and bacon in the 1 door reach-in. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Proper storage reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

Rice was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

Beef was observed thawing in the prep sink, in stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Beef was completely submerged under running water at the time of inspection. Proper thawing methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by live activity of baby cockroach and dead cockroach. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to increase pest control and have a pest control report available for inspector at the time of next inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026
Onions were observed stored on the floor in the basement area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026
Pots and pans were observed being stored on the floor in the kitchen. Pots, pans, and cooking equipment must be stored in a way to prevent contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026
The 3 door reach-in gaskets were observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/26/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the 1 door reach-in, fryolator, and stove top have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/12/2026
Inspector observed an accumulation of condensation throughout the establishment. PIC informed that proper ventilation is needed to remove excess moisture New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
Allergen statement is lacking on menu. Establishment must add allergen statement to menu. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

1 door reach-in was observed holding an ambient temperature of 55 DF and in poor repair. Cold-holding units must be able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Establishment is to have unit serviced. Steam table water was observed holding a temperature of 124 DF. Hot-holding units must be able to keep food at a temperature of 135 DF or higher. Establishment is to have unit serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025
Establishment is lacking proper drain plugs to hold water in the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain drain plugs. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Establishment is to install an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025
Establishment is lacking a dumpster/refuse area. Per PIC, establishment has a contract with dumpster company and dumpster will be delivered soon. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025
